Confluence.text: "con-" (together) + "flu" (flow) = rivers (or ideas) flowing together into one.

Conformist.text: "con-" (together) + "form" = taking the exact same form or shape as the rest of society.

Confound.text: Dumbfounded - you are so thoroughly confused you are left dumbfounded.

Congeal.text: Jell-O - it cools down and thickens into a solid mass, just like Jell-O.

Congenial.text: "con-" (with) + "gen" (nature) = sharing the same nature; highly agreeable and friendly.

Congregation.text: "con-" (together) + "greg" (flock) = a flock of people gathering together.

Congruity.text: Geometry - just like congruent triangles, everything fits perfectly together in agreement.

Connive.text: Knives - secretly plotting behind someone's back with knives.

Consensus.text: "con-" (together) + "sens" (feel) = everyone in the room feeling the same way; a general agreement.

Consign.text: Sign - you sign the paperwork to formally hand the goods over to someone else's care.

Consolation.text: Console - giving a sad gamer a new console to comfort them after a major loss.

Construe.text: Construct - you carefully construct the meaning of a complex sentence in your mind.

Consummate.text: "con-" (together) + "summa" (highest) = brought to the absolute highest, most complete level of skill.

Contemporaneous.text: "con-" (together) + "tempor" (time) = two events happening at the exact same time.

Contentious.text: Contender - a boxer who loves to fight and argue.

Contravene.text: "contra-" (against) + "venire" (come) = to come against a direct rule or law.

Contrite.text: From Latin contritus (ground to pieces). Your heart is crushed and ground to pieces by the guilt of what you've done.

Contusion.text: Confusion - getting hit in the head so hard it leaves a massive bruise and causes confusion.

Conundrum.text: Nun drum - a nun playing a heavy metal drum kit is a confusing, puzzling situation to figure out.

Convene.text: "con-" (together) + "venire" (come) = to officially come together for a meeting.

Convivial.text: "con-" (with) + "viv" (life) = full of life, highly festive, and sociable.

Convoluted.text: "con-" (together) + "volvere" (roll) = rolled and twisted together so many times it becomes impossibly complicated.

Copious.text: Copy us - the printer made so many copies you now have an abundant supply.

Cordial.text: "cord" (heart) = acting in a warm, polite, and heartfelt way.

Coronation.text: "coron" (crown) = the official ceremony of giving someone the crown.

Corpulence.text: "corp" (body) = having a massive, excessively fat body.

Corroborate.text: "cor-" (together) + "robor" (strength) = to add strength to a witness's story by providing matching evidence.

Cosmopolitan.text: "cosm" (world) + "polit" (citizen) = a citizen of the world; highly sophisticated and well-traveled.

Coup.text: Coop - the angry chickens violently overthrew the farmer to take control of the coop.

Covet.text: Cover it - you want your neighbor's new car so badly you throw a tarp over it to steal it.

Covert.text: Cover it - keeping the military operation completely covered up and secret.

Credulity.text: "cred" (believe) = an excessive readiness to believe things; extreme gullibility.

Cupidity.text: Named after Cupid, the Roman god of desire. While Cupid is associated with love, the word evolved to mean an extreme, greedy desire for…

Cursory.text: Cursor - moving your computer cursor quickly over the text without actually stopping to read it.

Curt.text: Cut - a spoken response so short and rude it feels like you've been cut off.

Curtail.text: Cut tail - to cut the tail off a long meeting to shorten it.

Daunting.text: Haunting - a ghost haunting your house is an incredibly intimidating and scary problem to deal with.

Dearth.text: Dead earth - a dead patch of earth has a severe scarcity and lack of crops.

Debacle.text: Da buckle - the buckle on the CEO's belt breaks during a keynote speech, causing a complete public disaster.

Debase.text: "de-" (down) + "base" = to lower the value, status, or quality of something.

Debauch.text: Da botch - you completely botched your life by engaging in extreme, corrupt partying.

Debunk.text: De-bunk - kicking your brother out of the top bunk because his claim that he called dibs is entirely false.

Decorous.text: Decorate - having excellent manners, perfectly presented like a beautifully decorated room.

Decry.text: Cry - crying out publicly against a policy you hate.

Deface.text: "de-" (down/remove) + "face" = to ruin the surface or outward appearance of something (like graffiti).

Defamatory.text: "de-" (remove) + "fame" = words specifically designed to destroy someone's fame or reputation.

Deferential.text: Reference - treating someone with such deep respect that you always use them as a reference.

Defile.text: File - spilling black coffee all over a clean file, completely corrupting and dirtying it.

Deft.text: Theft - a pickpocket must be incredibly quick and skillful to pull off a theft.

Defunct.text: "de-" (off) + "funct" (perform) = no longer functioning; dead or extinct.

Deleterious.text: Delete - a computer virus that is so harmful it deletes all your hard drive files.

Delineate.text: "de-" (out) + "line" = to trace the exact lines or outline of a plan in detail.

Demagogue.text: "dem" (people) + "agog" (leader) = a toxic political leader who manipulates the dark emotions of the people to gain power.

Demarcation.text: The mark - setting the physical mark on the ground to define a clear boundary.

Demean.text: "de-" (down) + "mean" = to put someone down and intentionally make them feel inferior.

Demure.text: Murmur - a shy, modest girl who only speaks in a quiet murmur.

Denigrate.text: "de-" (completely) + "nigr" (black) = to heavily blacken someone's reputation with insults.

Denounce.text: "de-" (down) + "nuntiare" (speak/announce) = to publicly announce your rejection of someone.

Depravity.text: Deprive - a villain completely deprived of any moral goodness.

Deprecate.text: Appreciate (the opposite) - to heavily express disapproval of something, the exact opposite of appreciating it.

Derelict.text: Dairy licked - the abandoned ice cream factory is so ruined that stray dogs have licked all the rusted dairy vats.

Deride.text: The ride - ruthlessly mocking the kid who is too scared to go on the roller coaster ride.

Desiccated.text: Desert - completely dried out and lifeless, exactly like a desert.

Desolate.text: "de-" (completely) + "sol" (alone) = completely left alone, empty, and barren.

Despondent.text: Respond - someone so hopelessly depressed they won't even respond to your texts.

Despot.text: Death pot - a cruel, absolute dictator who throws his enemies into a boiling death pot.

Destitute.text: Deserted - someone who has been entirely deserted by wealth, leaving them in extreme poverty.

Devious.text: "de-" (away) + "via" (way) = wandering away from the straight, honest path to be sneaky.

Diaphanous.text: "dia-" (through) + "phan" (show) = fabric so light and sheer that light shows right through it.

Didactic.text: Dictator - a teacher who constantly lectures you on morals like a strict dictator.

Diffident.text: "dis-" (not) + "fid" (trust) = lacking trust in your own abilities; highly shy and unconfident.

Diffuse.text: "dis-" (apart) + "fundere" (pour) = poured apart, spreading out thinly over a wide area.

Dilatory.text: Delay story - the lawyer told a long, pointless delay story just to waste time in court.

Dirge.text: Dirt - the slow, mournful song played while throwing dirt on a coffin.

Disaffected.text: "dis-" (not) + "affect" = no longer emotionally affected by or loyal to the leader.

Disavow.text: "dis-" (not) + "avow" (swear) = to swear you have absolutely no connection to something.

Disclose.text: "dis-" (un) + "close" = to un-close the vault and reveal a secret.

Discomfit.text: Discomfort - to make someone feel deeply embarrassed and in a state of discomfort.

Discordant.text: "dis-" (apart) + "cord" (heart) = hearts apart, creating harsh, clashing sounds or opinions.

Discursive.text: Cursive - a speech wandering off topic, looping all over the place just like cursive handwriting.

Disdain.text: This stain - looking with absolute snobby disgust at "this stain" on your brand new shirt.

Disgruntled.text: Grunt - an angry, dissatisfied worker walking away just grunting under his breath.

Disheartened.text: "dis-" (remove) + "heart" = having your courage or spirit entirely taken away.

Disparage.text: Spar - to verbally spar with someone just to belittle and insult their worth.

Disparate.text: Separate - two things that are so fundamentally different they must be kept totally separate.

Dispel.text: "dis-" (apart) + "pel" (drive) = to drive away a rumor or bad feeling.

Disperse.text: "dis-" (apart) + "spars" (scatter) = to scatter the crowd in different directions.

Disrepute.text: "dis-" (not) + "repute" (reputation) = held in extremely low esteem by the public.

Dissemble.text: Disassemble - to disassemble your true face and hide behind a fake, deceptive one.

Disseminate.text: "dis-" (apart) + "semin" (seed) = to scatter information widely, just like a farmer planting seeds.

Dissent.text: "dis-" (apart) + "sent" (feel) = to feel apart from the group; to officially disagree.

Dissipate.text: Sip it - to sip your drink so slowly over hours that the ice entirely melts and dissipates.

Dissonance.text: "dis-" (apart) + "son" (sound) = musical notes that clash aggressively and don't belong together.

Dissuade.text: "dis-" (against) + "suadere" (persuade) = to heavily persuade someone not to do something.

Distend.text: "dis-" (apart) + "tend" (stretch) = to stretch outwards, usually used for medical swelling.

Dither.text: Dizzy - spinning around dizzy, completely paralyzed and unable to make a decision.

Divine.text: Vine - predicting the future by reading the tangled growth of a magic vine.

Divulge.text: Bulge - the secret is bulging out of your pockets until you finally crack and reveal it.

Docile.text: "doc" (teach) = easily taught and completely submissive to handling.

Dogmatic.text: Dog - a dog guarding its bone is stubbornly attached to it, just like someone stubbornly attached to their unproven opinion.

Dormant.text: "dorm" (sleep) = currently sleeping or temporarily inactive (like a volcano).

Dour.text: Sour - having a sour, gloomy, and extremely severe facial expression.

Dubious.text: "dub" (doubt) = a claim filled with hesitation and serious doubt.

Duress.text: Stress - being put under extreme physical stress or threats to force a confession.

Ebullient.text: From Latin ebullire (to boil over). You are so enthusiastic and joyful that your energy literally boils over the pot.

Eclectic.text: Elect - electing to take the best individual pieces from a wide variety of different styles.

Edict.text: "e-" (out) + "dict" (speak) = an official, undeniable order spoken out by a king or authority.

Efface.text: "ex-" (out) + "face" = to completely wipe out or erase the face of a monument.

Effervescent.text: From Latin effervescere (to begin to boil). Used literally for fizzy drinks, and figuratively for bubbly, highly enthusiastic personalities.

Efficacious.text: Effective - highly successful in producing the exact desired result.

Effrontery.text: Front - having the brazen, arrogant nerve to get right up in someone's "front" (face).

Effulgent.text: Full gem - shining radiantly and brightly like a flawless, full gem.

Egregious.text: From Latin ex (out) + grege (flock). Standing out from the rest of the flock in a shockingly bad or horrific way.

Elegy.text: Allergy - writing a beautiful, sad poem for a friend who tragically died from a peanut allergy.

Elicit.text: Illegal - the detective trying to draw out a confession from someone who did something illegal.

Eloquent.text: "e-" (out) + "loqu" (speak) = speaking out in a beautifully fluent, persuasive manner.

Elucidate.text: "e-" (out) + "luc" (light) = to shine a spotlight on a complex topic to make it clear.

Elude.text: "e-" (out) + "ludere" (play) = to play a clever trick to escape the guards.

Emaciated.text: Mac (Macaroni) - someone who is so starving and bone-thin they desperately need a giant bowl of Mac and Cheese.

Embellish.text: Bell - decorating a plain, boring Christmas tree by hanging shiny bells all over it.

Embezzle.text: Bedazzle - the corrupt accountant stole company funds just to bedazzle her own wardrobe.

Emend.text: Amend - to make precise corrections and improvements to a written text.

Eminent.text: Eminem - a highly respected, famous, and prominent figure in their field.

Emollient.text: "e-" (out) + "mollire" (soften) = a lotion designed to completely soften the skin.

Empathy.text: "em-" (in) + "path" (feeling) = physically feeling into someone else's emotions.

Empirical.text: Empire - the Roman Empire was built on practical, observable facts and engineering, not just philosophical theories.

Emulate.text: Emulator - running a video game emulator on your PC to perfectly imitate an old console.

Enamor.text: "en-" (in) + "amor" (love) = to be entirely filled with a feeling of love and fascination.

Encumber.text: Cucumber - trying to run a marathon while carrying a 50-pound sack of cucumbers is a massive burden.

Enervate.text: Energy out - a disease that completely drains the energy out of your body.

Enfranchise.text: Franchise - giving a citizen the keys to the "franchise," meaning granting them the legal right to vote.

Engender.text: "en-" (in) + "gen" (birth) = to give birth to a situation or a feeling of trust.

Enigmatic.text: Enigma machine - highly mysterious and impossible to crack, just like the WWII code machine.

Enmity.text: Enemy - the intense, deeply rooted hatred you feel toward a bitter enemy.

Ennui.text: Are we? - "Are we there yet?" asked the kid in the backseat, suffering from extreme, soul-crushing boredom.

Enthrall.text: Originally meant to put someone in "thrall" (slavery). Now it means to capture someone's attention so completely that they are a willing…

Ephemeral.text: From Greek ephemeros (lasting only one day). Originally used by biologists for short-lived insects like mayflies, now means anything…

Epistolary.text: Pistol - writing a highly formal series of letters to threaten a duel with a pistol.

Epitome.text: Epic tome - an epic, massive tome that serves as the perfect, flawless example of fantasy literature.

Equanimity.text: "equ" (equal) + "anim" (mind/spirit) = keeping an even, perfectly balanced mind under intense stress.

Equivocal.text: "equi" (equal) + "voc" (voice) = a politician speaking with two equal voices, making their stance completely ambiguous and unclear.

Erudite.text: "e-" (out) + "rud" (raw/rude) = trained completely out of a raw, ignorant state into profound scholarly knowledge.

Eschew.text: Ah - choo!-you sneeze, and I deliberately avoid you to escape the germs.

Esoteric.text: Isolated - knowledge that is so highly isolated that only a tiny group of experts actually understand it.

Espouse.text: Spouse - to marry yourself to a political idea or belief, defending it just like you would a spouse.

Ethereal.text: "ether" (upper air) = light, airy, and belonging entirely to the heavens rather than earth.

Euphoric.text: "eu-" (good) + "pherein" (bear) = bearing a feeling of intense, overwhelmingly good happiness.

Evanescent.text: "e-" (out) + "vanescere" (vanish) = fading rapidly out of sight; vanishing like vapor.

Evince.text: Evidence - to clearly show evidence or proof of a hidden feeling.

Exacerbate.text: Extra bad - making a bad situation extra bad by interfering.

Exalt.text: Exhale - exhaling in pure awe as you praise and elevate a hero to a high rank.

Exasperate.text: Ex-aspirin - you have a splitting headache and realize you are out of aspirin, which thoroughly annoys and frustrates you.

Excavate.text: "ex-" (out) + "cav" (cave) = to hollow out a cave or systematically dig historical artifacts out of the ground.

Exculpate.text: "ex-" (out) + "culp" (blame) = to officially take the blame off someone's record.

Execrable.text: Excrement - a movie so utterly foul and detestable it belongs in the sewer with the excrement.

Exhort.text: Exhaust - cheering and urging your team on so intensely from the sidelines that you exhaust yourself.

Exigent.text: Exit gent - the building is currently on fire, so it is highly urgent and demanding that the gentleman exits immediately.

Exonerate.text: "ex-" (out) + "onus" (burden) = taking the heavy burden of guilt permanently off someone's shoulders.

Exorbitant.text: "ex-" (out) + "orbit" = a price tag so insanely high it has flown completely out of orbit.

Expedient.text: "ex-" (out) + "ped" (foot) = originally meaning to free a foot from a trap; doing what is most convenient and practical to solve a problem…

Expiate.text: Expiration - the pirate must do good deeds to make amends for his crimes before his expiration date.

Expunge.text: Sponge - using a massive sponge to completely wipe away and erase a criminal record.

Expurgate.text: Purge - the school board deciding to purge a classic book of all its offensive content before assigning it.

Extant.text: X-tent - the ancient explorer's tent still miraculously stands; it currently exists and survived the storm.

Extol.text: Toll - paying the toll bridge operator with highly enthusiastic praise instead of actual money.

Extraneous.text: "extra" (outside) = coming from the outside and therefore entirely irrelevant to the core subject.

Extricate.text: Trick - finally figuring out the puzzle's trick to free yourself from the trap.

Exult.text: Vault-pole - vaulting high into the air out of sheer triumph and joy after winning gold.

Facile.text: "fac" (do/make) = an argument that is so easy to make that it is superficial and entirely lacks depth.

Fallacious.text: "fall" (deceive) = based on a false, highly deceitful premise.

Fastidious.text: Fast tidier - obsessing over every tiny detail; nothing sloppy gets past.

Fathom.text: Originally a nautical unit of measurement for water depth (about 6 feet). To fathom a problem is to successfully reach the very bottom of…

Fatuous.text: Tattoo us - two drunk, incredibly silly friends shouting "Tattoo us!" at 3 AM; a foolish, pointless idea.

Fecund.text: Feces - putting cow feces (manure) on the soil makes it highly fertile and incredibly productive.

Felicitous.text: Fleece - finding a warm fleece jacket in your trunk right as a blizzard hits is a highly fortunate, well-timed event.

Feral.text: Fur all - a stray animal covered in fur all over, acting completely wild and untamed.

Fervent.text: Fever - having a burning, passionate fever for a political cause.

Fetid.text: Feet - a swamp smelling as horrific as a rotting pair of unwashed feet.

Fetter.text: Feet - putting heavy iron chains on a prisoner's feet to severely restrict them.

Fickle.text: Pickle - you can't decide if you want a sweet or a sour pickle because your tastes change constantly and unpredictably.

Flaccid.text: Flat - a tire that is completely flat, limp, and entirely lacking firmness.

Flagrant.text: Flag - a foul on the basketball court so obviously bad and intentional the referee throws a giant penalty flag.

Florid.text: "flor" (flower) = writing that is heavily decorated and excessively flowery.

Flout.text: Fly out - to openly mock and break the rules, then fly out of the country to avoid arrest.

Foil.text: Aluminum foil - wrapping the villain's evil plans in heavy aluminum foil to completely block and ruin them.

Forbearance.text: Bear - having the immense patience and self-control not to shoot the bear when it crosses your path.

Forestall.text: Before stall - placing a heavy stall in the road before the enemy arrives to entirely prevent their advance.

Forlorn.text: Torn - your heart is totally torn because you are pitifully sad, lonely, and abandoned.

Fortitude.text: "fort" (strong) = immense mental strength and courage while enduring deep pain.

Fortuitous.text: "fortun" (chance) = something great happening by a lucky, happy accident rather than design.

Fractious.text: "fract" (break) = a group that is easily broken into arguments; highly irritable and unruly.

Fraught.text: Caught - a tense situation where you are constantly worried you will be caught because the room is filled with danger.

Frenetic.text: Frenzy - moving with wild, incredibly fast, frantic energy.

Frivolous.text: Free volley - playing a completely meaningless, silly free volley in tennis instead of focusing on the actual match.

Frugal.text: Fruit girl - the girl who only eats free fruit from the trees to save money because she is highly economical.

Furtive.text: Fur thief - the fur thief moves quietly and secretly through the mansion to avoid being caught.

Garish.text: Garnish - piling way too much cheap, brightly colored garnish on a plate, making the whole meal look tacky.

Garrulous.text: Gary rules - Gary never shuts up about how much he rules; an excessively talkative guy.

Genial.text: "gen" (nature/born) = having a naturally friendly, warm, and cheerful disposition.

Goad.text: Goat - poking a stubborn goat repeatedly with a sharp stick to provoke it to move.

Gourmand.text: Gourmet man - a man who loves eating rich, gourmet food in excessive amounts.
